headline: Israel FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — day-3 ceasefire-window-close floor conduct documentation update. No score change (floor). OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations from flotilla detention (35 individuals documented May 8 OHCHR press release) REMAIN UNADDRESSED at day-3 close — deportation of last detained activists (Abukeshek, Avila, May 10) closed the detention-duration phase but DOES NOT resolve the conduct-during-detention accountability category. Gaza casualty accumulation continues — 72,740+ confirmed killed (20,179 children) per Palestinian Ministry of Health as of May 11; airstrikes, shelling, and gunfire across all Gaza areas per UNRWA Situation Report 220. UNRWA access block continues — since March 2025 Israel has blocked UNRWA from directly bringing humanitarian personnel and aid into Gaza. No floor-exit criteria progress. Floor conduct documentation appended for the 3-day ceasefire-window-close period.

conduct_documentation.summary: Day-3 ceasefire-window-close floor conduct documentation update. Three open conduct categories remain unaddressed: (1) OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations (35-individual cluster); (2) UNRWA access block (since March 2025); (3) Gaza casualty accumulation (72,740+ confirmed killed).
conduct_documentation.may_11_conduct_continuation: Day-3 conduct continuation across the three open categories:

ohchr_torture_inhumane_treatment_allegat.summary: OHCHR press release May 8 documented torture/inhumane-treatment allegations involving 35 individuals from the April 29 flotilla interdiction. The May 10 deportation of Abukeshek and Avila closed the detention-duration phase but DOES NOT resolve the conduct-during-detention accountability category. No accountability action announced; no investigation opened; no remedy provided.
ohchr_torture_inhumane_treatment_allegat.evidentiary_distinction: 'Resolved by deportation' ≠ 'accountability achieved.' Deportation removes the detainees but does not constitute remedy, investigation, or acknowledgment of the underlying alleged violations.

unrwa_access_block.summary: UNRWA Situation Report 220 confirms that since March 2025 Israel has blocked UNRWA from directly bringing humanitarian personnel and aid into Gaza. This is a 14-month structural access-restriction. Day-3 close: access block continues.
unrwa_access_block.evidentiary_distinction: Structural humanitarian-access conduct, distinct from individual operations. Per UNRWA, this restriction is a category-level barrier to humanitarian operations affecting the agency responsible for ~70% of Gaza's pre-war humanitarian support architecture.

gaza_casualty_accumulation.status: ONGOING — confirmed 72,740+ killed (20,179 children) as of May 11 per Palestinian Ministry of Health (referenced via UNRWA/OCHA OPT)
gaza_casualty_accumulation.summary: Casualty data continues to accumulate. Day-3 close: airstrikes, shelling, gunfire continue across all Gaza areas per UNRWA Situation Report 220. No ceasefire arrangement for Gaza in window (the May 9-11 trilateral ceasefire was Ukraine-Russia-US, not Gaza).
gaza_casualty_accumulation.evidentiary_distinction: Primary floor anchor for Israel. Ongoing operational conduct, not episodic.

closed_categories: April 29 – May 10 arbitrary-detention duration event (Abukeshek, Avila): resolved by deportation May 10
open_categories: Gaza military operations conduct (primary floor anchor) — 72,740+ confirmed killed (20,179 children) as of May 11
UNRWA access block (since March 2025) — 14-month structural humanitarian-access restriction
OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations (35-individual cluster, May 8 press release) — unresolved
UNCLOS jurisdiction question (April 29 maritime interception at 500 nautical miles in international waters) — unanswered
UNCAT accountability for reported mistreatment during detention — no investigation announced

conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Day-3 close: three open conduct categories continue unaddressed; one detention-duration category closed by deportation. No floor-exit progress. The accumulation of unresolved conduct categories continues to entrench the floor placement. Floor-exit criteria would require acknowledgment, investigation, or remedy on at least one of the unresolved categories; none of these occurred in window.

key_evidence: May 11 2026 (in-window, day-3 close): OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations from flotilla detention (35 individuals, May 8 OHCHR press release) REMAIN UNADDRESSED. No Israeli accountability action announced in window.

May 11 2026 (in-window): UNRWA Situation Report 220 documents continuing humanitarian crisis in Gaza Strip and occupied West Bank; airstrikes, shelling, and gunfire across all Gaza areas; civilian casualties continuing.

May 11 2026 (in-window): UNRWA access block continues — since March 2025 Israel has blocked UNRWA from directly bringing humanitarian personnel and aid into Gaza. 14-month structural access-restriction at day-3 close.
May 11 2026 (in-window): 72,740+ confirmed killed in Gaza (20,179 children) per Palestinian Ministry of Health (via OCHA OPT casualty tracking). Day-3 casualty accumulation continues.
May 10 2026 (carry-over): Deportation of Abukeshek and Avila closed detention-duration event but does not resolve conduct-during-detention accountability.

evidence.source: UNRWA Situation Report 220: humanitarian crisis in Gaza Strip and occupied West Bank
evidence.finding: Day-3 close: airstrikes, shelling, and gunfire across all Gaza areas; UNRWA humanitarian-access block continues since March 2025; civilian casualties continuing.
72,740+ confirmed killed in Gaza (20,179 children) as of May 11 per Palestinian Ministry of Health. Day-3 casualty accumulation continues.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). State suppresses independent monitoring of detainee conditions; OHCHR access remains constrained; UNRWA access blocked.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 unchanged (floor). Detention-phase mistreatment allegations unresolved; Gaza casualty accumulation continues (72,740+); deportation does not constitute empathic response.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Deportation is a release-from-detention action but not an accountability action; UNRWA access block is a continuing humanitarian-access conduct barrier.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). Continued floor-status conduct on civilian-protection norms in Gaza operations.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). UNCLOS jurisdiction unanswered; UNRWA structural-access-restriction continues; B3 scope-clarity floor-active.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). No accountability action announced for torture allegations; AB1 harm-acknowledgment floor active; AB4 accountability-to-external-norms floor active.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 unchanged (floor). No structural-reform signals; floor sustained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). Deportation without charges contradicts initial 'terrorist affiliation' framing — internal-consistency violation; INT floor active.

math_check.validator_check: Floor sustained. Composite cannot move below floor. Floor conduct record updated.

negativeSignals: OHCHR torture allegations unaddressed (35-individual cluster); UNRWA access block continuing (14 months since March 2025); Gaza casualty accumulation (72,740+ confirmed killed, 20,179 children); UNCLOS jurisdiction unanswered; UNCAT accountability not pursued; no Israeli corrective response documented in window across any open conduct category.
positiveSignals: None identified in window for Israel as state actor. (Civil-society advocacy continuing; detainees' post-deportation statements about continuing advocacy preserve the activist record but are not Israeli-state positive signals.)
sector_context: Floor cluster. Israel's day-3 close conduct record: one detention-duration category closed (May 10 deportation); five conduct categories open and unaddressed (Gaza operations, UNRWA access block, OHCHR torture allegations, UNCLOS jurisdiction, UNCAT accountability). The accumulation of unresolved conduct categories continues to entrench the floor placement.
open_actions_resolved: Day-3 ceasefire-window-close floor conduct documentation update logged
Open conduct categories enumerated at day-3 close (Gaza casualties, UNRWA access block, OHCHR torture allegations, UNCLOS, UNCAT)
Closed category logged: April 29 – May 10 arbitrary-detention duration event (resolved by deportation)
open_actions_carried: Watch: any Israeli response to OHCHR May 8 torture allegations (35-individual cluster)
Watch: UNCLOS jurisdiction follow-up by Spanish, Swedish, Brazilian governments
Watch: UNRWA access block evolution (14 months since March 2025)
Watch: Gaza operations conduct (primary floor anchor) — casualty accumulation continuing
methodology_consistency_check: Day-3 close conduct update applies the same floor-protocol rules used May 5-10: floor entities receive conduct documentation without composite movement; closure of one violation category does not constitute floor-exit progress unless accountability is achieved. Confirmed methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
